name: Old-Fashioned Butterscotch Candy
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a 2-quart heavy saucepan.
2. Stir and cook over medium heat until the sugar is dissolved.
3. Reduce heat and cook at a medium boil, stirring as needed to control foaming and avoid sticking as the mixture thickens.
4. If sugar crystals form on the sides of the pan, wipe them off.
5. Cook to the hard crack stage (300°F).
6. Remove from heat and let stand 1 minute.
7. Meanwhile, butter two sheets of aluminum foil and place them on baking sheets.
8. Quickly drop teaspoonfuls of butterscotch onto the foil, making patties about 1 inch in diameter. Space them 1/2 inch apart.
9. If the candy thickens so that it will not drop easily, set the pan in hot water until it is again workable.
10. Makes about 6 dozen patties or 1-1/4 pounds of butterscotch.
